sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The default animation shows the yellow object staying at (7, 44) for all 6 frames - no movement at all. This is different from Level 1 where the object moved down and laterally. 
Looking at the bottom boxes, I see two preview boxes:
- Left box (blue border): shows yellow dots (representing some pattern)
- Right box (gray border): shows a yellow square on black background
The left panel has a charcoal shape and buttons. The right panel has a yellow L-shape, a yellow square, and buttons.
I think the two boxes at the bottom are "preview" boxes showing what happens when you click the blue blob. The left blue-bordered box shows the left panel's preview, and the gray-bordered box shows the right panel's preview.
Wait, looking at the bottom boxes again:
- Left box (rows 54-62, cols 7-15): blue border, yellow dots inside at rows 57-59, cols 8-9
- Right box (rows 55-61, cols 17-23): gray border, yellow square at rows 55-56
Actually, I think the bottom boxes might be clickable elements that show the current configuration. Let me look at the left panel more carefully.
The left panel has:
- A charcoal shape at rows 16-19, cols 22-25
- 4 horizontal black bars at row 33
- 4 vertical gray bars at rows 35-49
The right panel has:
- A yellow L-shape at rows 7-11, cols 44-49
- A yellow square at rows 24-27, cols 45-48
- 4 horizontal gray bars at row 33
- 4 vertical gray bars at rows 35-49
I think the buttons on the left control the left panel's animation, and the buttons on the right control the right panel's animation. The charcoal shape in the left panel might be the "target" that the yellow shape needs to match.
Let me try toggling some buttons on the right panel to see what happens. Since there are 4 buttons per panel (instead of 3 in Level 1), the animation might have more steps.
Let me try turning OFF one of the right panel's horizontal buttons and see what changes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try toggling the first right-panel horizontal button (gray at row 33, cols 38-40)
# to see what it does
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 39}])  # Toggle right H button 1
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 46}])  # Run animation

if last_animation:
    for i, entry in enumerate(last_animation):
        for j, frame in enumerate(entry.frames):
            seg = frame.segmentation
            y_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and min(r for r,c in n['boundary']) < 30]
            if y_nodes:
                n = y_nodes[0]
                print(f"Frame {j}: top-left=({n['boundary'][0][0]}, {n['boundary'][0][1]}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
